entries.summary: EU member states agreed on the Council's general approach to the AI Act.
European Parliament committees backed a stricter draft text adding general-purpose AI rules.
European Parliament adopted its negotiating position on the AI Act in plenary.
Negotiators reached a political deal on the world's first comprehensive AI law.
Officials hailed the AI Act as a global benchmark for regulating artificial intelligence.
France, Germany and Italy lifted objections that had threatened the deal's text.
EU member states' permanent representatives unanimously endorsed the negotiated AI Act text.
Two key European Parliament committees approved the AI Act ahead of plenary.
European Parliament passed the AI Act with 523 votes in favour.
European Commission set up an AI Office to oversee enforcement under the new law.
Council of the European Union gave final approval to the AI Act.
AI Act was signed by the presidents of the Parliament and the Council.
AI Act was published in the Official Journal of the European Union.
AI Act formally entered into force across the European Union's member states.
European AI Office released a draft general-purpose AI code of practice for industry.
Initial AI Act bans on prohibited practices and AI-literacy obligations took effect.
AI Act rules on general-purpose AI models began applying across the European Union.
EU member states agreed on the Council's negotiating position on the AI Act draft.
Two European Parliament committees voted to tighten the AI Act's text on foundation models.
European Parliament backed the AI Act, opening negotiations with member governments.
Reports said France, Germany and Italy were resisting strict foundation-model rules.
EU officials announced a provisional deal on the bloc's landmark artificial-intelligence law.
France, Germany and Italy formally dropped objections clearing a key procedural hurdle.
EU ambassadors gave unanimous backing to the final compromise AI Act text.
European Parliament's lead committees approved the final draft of the AI Act.
European Parliament adopted the AI Act, the first comprehensive AI law of its kind.
EU Council formally approved the AI Act, completing the legislative process.
Presidents of the Parliament and the Council signed the AI Act into law.
AI Act was published in the Official Journal, fixing the timing of obligations.
AI Act came into force, beginning a phased rollout of its obligations.
Reports said major US companies were lobbying to soften draft AI Act guidelines.
European AI Office circulated the first draft code of practice for general-purpose AI.
Earliest AI Act provisions on banned practices and AI literacy began applying.
AI Act rules on general-purpose AI models took effect, including transparency duties.
